speaker:
FIRST
               
              WATCH
text: Come on, my masters, each man take his stand.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WATCH
text: The King by this is set him down to sleep.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              WATCH
text: What, will he not to bed?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WATCH
text: Why, no, for he hath made a solemn vow

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WATCH
text: Never to lie and take his natural rest

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WATCH
text: Till Warwick or himself be quite suppressed.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              WATCH
text: Tomorrow, then, belike shall be the day,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              WATCH
text: If Warwick be so near as men report.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              WATCH
text: But say, I pray, what nobleman is that

speaker:
THIRD
               
              WATCH
text: That with the King here resteth in his tent?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WATCH
text: ’Tis the Lord Hastings, the King’s chiefest friend.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              WATCH
text: O, is it so? But why commands the King

speaker:
THIRD
               
              WATCH
text: That his chief followers lodge in towns about him,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              WATCH
text: While he himself keeps in the cold field?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              WATCH
text: ’Tis the more honor, because more dangerous.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              WATCH
text: Ay, but give me worship and quietness;

speaker:
THIRD
               
              WATCH
text: I like it better than a dangerous honor.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              WATCH
text: If Warwick knew in what estate he stands,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              WATCH
text: ’Tis to be doubted he would waken him.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WATCH
text: Unless our halberds did shut up his passage.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              WATCH
text: Ay, wherefore else guard we his royal tent

speaker:
SECOND
               
              WATCH
text: But to defend his person from night foes?

speaker: WARWICK
text: This is his tent, and see where stand his guard.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Courage, my masters. Honor, now or never!

speaker: WARWICK
text: But follow me, and Edward shall be ours.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WATCH
text: Who goes there?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              WATCH
text: Stay, or thou diest!

speaker: SOMERSET
text: What are they that fly there?

speaker: WARWICK
text: Richard and Hastings.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Let them go. Here is the Duke.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: The Duke?

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Why, Warwick, when we parted, thou call’dst me king.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Ay, but the case is altered.

speaker: WARWICK
text: When you disgraced me in my embassade,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Then I degraded you from being king

speaker: WARWICK
text: And come now to create you Duke of York.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Alas, how should you govern any kingdom

speaker: WARWICK
text: That know not how to use ambassadors,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Nor how to be contented with one wife,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Nor how to use your brothers brotherly,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Nor how to study for the people’s welfare,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Nor how to shroud yourself from enemies?

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Yea, brother of Clarence, art thou here too?

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Nay, then, I see that Edward needs must down.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Yet, Warwick, in despite of all mischance,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Of thee thyself and all thy complices,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Edward will always bear himself as king.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Though Fortune’s malice overthrow my state,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: My mind exceeds the compass of her wheel.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Then for his mind be Edward England’s king,

speaker: WARWICK
text: But Henry now shall wear the English crown

speaker: WARWICK
text: And be true king indeed, thou but the shadow.—

speaker: WARWICK
text: My lord of Somerset, at my request,

speaker: WARWICK
text: See that forthwith Duke Edward be conveyed

speaker: WARWICK
text: Unto my brother, Archbishop of York.

speaker: WARWICK
text: When I have fought with Pembroke and his fellows,

speaker: WARWICK
text: I’ll follow you and tell what answer

speaker: WARWICK
text: Lewis and the Lady Bona send to him.—

speaker: WARWICK
text: Now for awhile farewell, good Duke of York.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: What Fates impose, that men must needs abide;

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: It boots not to resist both wind and tide.

speaker: OXFORD
text: What now remains, my lords, for us to do

speaker: OXFORD
text: But march to London with our soldiers?

speaker: WARWICK
text: Ay, that’s the first thing that we have to do,

speaker: WARWICK
text: To free King Henry from imprisonment

speaker: WARWICK
text: And see him seated in the regal throne.
